svg.text: 15.10 · The backup that won't restore — the catastrophe REALIZED
M13/13.5's warning, realized: data gone AND recovery gone · entirely preventable by tested restores
"backup SUCCEEDED" ✓ every night for a year — based on "did it RUN", NOT "can it RESTORE"
so silent failures accumulate UNDETECTED → discovered only when you try to restore (during the disaster)
DISASTER → you go to restore → it DOESN'T WORK
✗ CORRUPT (silent bit-rot, a backup-process bug)
✗ INCOMPLETE (a table silently excluded)
✗ UN-DECRYPTABLE (the key was lost/rotated, 13.14)
✗ MISSING the binlog (no PITR, 15.7)
→ data gone AND recovery gone
PREVENTION (the only one): TESTED RESTORES (M13/13.5)
automated restore DRILLS → scratch instance
→ apply PITR → VERIFY (checksums + reconciliation)
→ catches EVERY silent failure BEFORE the disaster
+ multiple backup types · off-site · key management
"you don't have backups, you have RESTORES"
mid-disaster recovery (damage limitation): try other backups / a replica / a logical dump · salvage (force_recovery, 15.6)
· reconstruct from external processor records (M12/12.14) — there's NO clean recovery, which is the point
💰 VERDICT: money PERMANENTLY LOST — the operational catastrophe, ENTIRELY preventable by tested restores
"backup succeeded" ≠ "restore works" — test it, or discover during the disaster that you have neither · THE M13/13.5 cautionary tale
